yesterdayAnalysis: Completed a solid 26-mile indoor bike (TSS 50, HR 111 avg) followed by a short 1-mile brick run (TSS 9, HR 138), demonstrating good discipline on the planned midweek brick. Heart rates were appropriately controlled — bike stayed well below threshold at 111 bpm, and the brick run HR of 138 shows good neuromuscular work without excessive stress. This was the appropriate scaled-down brick after Tuesday's 10.2-mile long run.
todayRecommendation: Honor the planned REST DAY completely — no swimming, biking, or running. Focus on walking, mobility work, hydration, and quality nutrition to absorb Tuesday's 10-miler and Wednesday's brick before Friday's swim and Saturday's critical long ride.
weekProgress: Week 8 of 10: Currently at 289 TSS of 500 target (58%) with 3 days remaining including today's rest day. This is perfectly on-track given the heavily back-loaded schedule — Saturday's long ride and Sunday's run will deliver the bulk of remaining TSS. keyInsight: The strategic move of the long run to Tuesday night is paying off — you're properly rested mid-week and positioned for the peak weekend volume that defines this build phase.
positives: Successfully completed 10.2-mile long run at 144 bpm average — first long run back and well-controlled HR
